description:
You are helping a cloud-migration architect rationalise the legacy folder layout of a small web stack.  
    The legacy code lives in two **real directories** that already exist on disk:

        1. /home/user/services/api
        2. /home/user/services/web

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Create a new parent directory called /home/user/current_release  
       (create it if it does not yet exist).  
    2. Inside /home/user/current_release create **symbolic links** named api and web that point to
       the real directories listed above.  
    3. Generate one plain-text log file recording the success of the operation.

    Exact log file specification  
    ----------------------------  
    • Full path of the log file: /home/user/migration_logs/symlink_report.log  
    • One line **per** symbolic link, in the following exact format (including spaces and the word “linked” with a lowercase “l”):  

          <LINK_PATH> -> <TARGET_PATH> : linked

      – No additional fields, timestamps, or blank lines.  
      – Lines must appear in this exact order:

            /home/user/current_release/api -> /home/user/services/api : linked
            /home/user/current_release/web -> /home/user/services/web : linked

    4. Make sure the log file’s directory (/home/user/migration_logs) exists; create it if needed.  
    5. When you are finished, you should be able to run the following command and obtain the **exact** output shown here (two lines, no trailing spaces):

          cat /home/user/migration_logs/symlink_report.log
          /home/user/current_release/api -> /home/user/services/api : linked
          /home/user/current_release/web -> /home/user/services/web : linked

    Constraints & hints  
    • You have normal user privileges (no sudo).  
    • There are only 3–4 terminal commands required:  
         – mkdir (possibly two of them)  
         – ln with the ‑s flag  
         – echo or printf to write the log.  
    • Do not remove or move the original /home/user/services/* directories; just create symlinks that reference them.  
    • Be mindful of quoting/spacing so the log file lines are exactly as specified.  

    Success criteria (checked automatically)  
    1. /home/user/current_release/api is a symbolic link that resolves to /home/user/services/api  
    2. /home/user/current_release/web is a symbolic link that resolves to /home/user/services/web  
    3. /home/user/migration_logs/symlink_report.log exists and contains precisely the two lines shown above, with no extra whitespace or blank lines.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing filesystem state (before agent starts)
    -->
    <preexisting_directories>
        /home/user/services
        /home/user/services/api
        /home/user/services/web
    </preexisting_directories>

    <preexisting_files>
        <!-- Small placeholder files to prove the directories are real -->
        /home/user/services/api/README.txt : "API service code lives here\n"
        /home/user/services/web/README.txt : "Web frontend code lives here\n"
    </preexisting_files>

    <!--
        Desired final state after agent completes the task
    -->
    <expected_directories>
        /home/user/current_release
        /home/user/migration_logs
    </expected_directories>

    <expected_symlinks>
        /home/user/current_release/api -> /home/user/services/api
        /home/user/current_release/web -> /home/user/services/web
    </expected_symlinks>

    <expected_files>
        /home/user/migration_logs/symlink_report.log :
            "/home/user/current_release/api -> /home/user/services/api : linked\n/home/user/current_release/web -> /home/user/services/web : linked\n"
    </expected_files>
name: task_000000_e7cbd114
